rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Consolidation Of Actions
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Defendants Brooke Wentz, Seven Seas Music, Inc. and The Rights Workshop, Inc. (collectively, the "Defendants")'s Motion for Consolidation of Actions (the "Motion") to consolidate a civil action initiated against them by Plaintiffs Lydia Wentz and Scott Wentz in San Francisco Superior Court case no. CGC-24-614512 (the "Civil Action"), with a probate proceeding initiated by Petitioners Lydia Wentz and Scott Wentz against Respondent Brooke Wentz individually and as Trustee and beneficiary of the Wentz Family Living Trust dated March 14, 2011 (the "Trust"), in San Francisco Superior Court case no. PTR-24-306958 (the "Probate Action"). The Motion is GRANTED for trial purposes only for the reasons set forth below. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice of the complaint filed in the Civil Action (the "Civil Complaint") and the petition filed in the Probate Action (the "Probate Petition") is GRANTED pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code secs. 450 and 452. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 1048, subd. (a), the court may order a joint hearing or trial of any or all matters at issue in pending actions when they involve a common question of law or fact. The decision to grant or deny a motion to consolidate is within the court's discretion. Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1149. A party or the court on its own motion can make a motion to consolidate. Nissan Motor Corp. v. Superior Court (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 150, 153.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of And Motion For Consolidation Of Actions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) The Civil Action seeks redress against Defendants Brooke Wentz, Seven Seas Music, Inc., and The Rights Workshop, Inc. for their alleged misappropriation of assets owned by Lydia Wentz and the Trust between 2017 and 2024. (Civil Complaint, at p.5:13 - p.7:25). Notably, Defendants Seven Seas Music, Inc. and The Rights Workshop, Inc. are corporate entities ostensibly "owned and/or controlled" by Defendant Brooke Wentz. (Opposition of Plaintiffs Lydia Seebach Wentz and Scott Wentz to Defendants' Motion for Consolidation of Actions, at p. 2:15-16). The Probate Action seeks redress against Brooke Wentz individually and as Trustee and beneficiary of the Trust for her alleged misappropriation of assets owned by Lydia Wentz and the Trust between 2018 and 2024. (Probate Petition, at p.4:24 - p.7:8). The primary question common to both matters is whether Brooke Wentz misappropriated assets owned by Lydia Wentz and/or the Trust. The matters differ in that the gravamen of the Probate Action seeks to hold Brooke Wentz responsible for alleged breaches of her fiduciary duty by taking assets owned by Lydia Wentz and/or the Trust, whereas the gravamen of the Civil Action seeks to hold Seven Seas Music, Inc., The Rights Workshop, Inc., and Brooke Wentz responsible for allegedly taking and receiving assets owned by Lydia Wentz and/or the Trust. In sum, the common question of fact in both actions is whether Brooke Wentz misappropriated assets, either individually, as a fiduciary of Lydia Wentz, or as an agent or officer of Seven Seas Music, Inc. and The Rights Workshop, Inc. Thus, consolidation is appropriate since the cases present essentially the same evidence, separate trials will pose the possibility of conflicting results, and consolidation will save time and money for the litigants and the court without undue prejudice to any litigant. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DANIKA GEBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Directing Investment Of Funds
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether to grant the petition as prayed or to require petitioner to open a guardianship estate to receive the funds. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARLAN SEE TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 11, 1990, AS RESTATED APRIL 14, 2010 AND AS AMENDED APRIL 11,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: 1. Order Compelling Trustee To Perform Duties Pursuant To Trust; 2. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Initial Successor Trustee; 3.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/2024. 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) The name and address of each beneficiary must be alleged in the petition. 3) The proof of service attached to the DE-120 form did not list Beverly Johnson, Horizons Foundation, and some specific devisees removed by the first amendment. If any individual beneficiary is deceased, please provide his/her date of death. 4) Melinda (or Malinda) Randolph's consent to act as successor trustee is not on file. Please confirm the first name, as the operative trust documents refer to "Malinda", while the petition refers to "Melinda". 5) Please provide a description and market value of each asset currently held by the trust, together with estimated annual income therefrom, for the purpose of fixing the successor trustee's bond. 6) Please provide the settlor's date of death. 7) Please provide a copy of a trustee notification under Probate Code section 16061.7, if served by the respondent trustee. 8) The proposed order includes a finding that the respondent is deemed to have predeceased the settlor, but the petition and prayer for relief did not.
